data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion G1G2 (Edit-set / scope accuracy) [agent]. Tool-grounded against the shipped eval seam (src/rebar/llm/eval.py), the packaged specs under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/, and the gating tests. Edit targets and CREATION scope are mostly sound; THREE grounded findings (two name-mismatches that would mis-target the registry, one unenumerated-consumer / blast-radius gap that the proposed validate_scorer change actually breaks).

AFFIRMATIONS (G1G2 criteria this chunk satisfies):
- Named edit targets EXIST: `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` (eval.py:1), `validate_eval_spec` (eval.py:191), `validate_scorer` (eval.py:107), and `tests/unit/` all exist and resolve. The proposed alternative `src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py` is a CREATION (new file, new behavior) and is correctly framed as new, not an edit to an existing symbol.
- Behavioral classification is honest: the registry + dataset-case schema are net-new behavior (CREATION); extending validate_scorer to reject unregistered deterministic names is an in-scope edit to an existing symbol. No hallucinated module path.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Fix the scorer-name list to match the names the SHIPPED specs actually use — `emits_valid_verdict` and `no_false_fail_*` do not exist in the codebase, and the registry must instead cover the real names or the offline gate will mis-target. A Grep across src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ shows the actual deterministic scorer names are: emits_valid_findings, no_fire_on_good_cases, recall_on_seeded_defects (finder); emits_valid_verification (NOT emits_valid_verdict), discriminates_true_from_false, no_sycophancy_on_real_defects (verifier); emits_valid_findings, recall_on_silent_drop, no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope (isf-finder); emits_valid_review_result, cites_real_paths (code-quality). `emits_valid_verdict` (search: zero hits) and `no_false_fail_*` (zero hits) are hallucinated edit targets; conversely `discriminates_true_from_false` and `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` are real shipped scorers the plan's name list OMITS — a registry built from the plan's list would (a) register a name no spec uses and (b) fail to register two names two specs DO use.
  - criteria: [G1G2]
  - location: plan "What" §1 ("the names used by ALL 7 specs: emits_valid_findings/emits_valid_review_result/emits_valid_verdict, recall_on_*, no_fire_on_*/no_false_fail_*, cites_real_paths"); AC line 1 ("every scorer name used by the 7 specs")
  - evidence: Grep "emits_valid_verdict" → zero hits; actual verifier name is `emits_valid_verification` (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:16). Grep "no_false_fail" → zero hits. Real verifier scorers `discriminates_true_from_false` (verifier.eval.yaml:19) and `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` (verifier.eval.yaml:26) are absent from the plan's enumeration.
  - suggested_fix: Replace `emits_valid_verdict`→`emits_valid_verification`, drop `no_false_fail_*`, and add `discriminates_true_from_false` + `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` to the registry coverage list.

- [ ] Reconcile the "ALL 7 specs" / "the 7 specs" count with the codebase — only FOUR eval specs exist (`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`: plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-isf-finder, code-quality), and README.plan-review.md enumerates only the 3 plan-review specs. "7" is unverifiable as written and risks the registry being scoped to a phantom spec set (it likely conflates the 7 plan-review *reviewer prompts/criteria-overlays* or the FP1–FP7 taxonomy with eval *specs*). State the actual spec set the registry must cover (the 4 shipped specs, or name the additional 3 if they are net-new artifacts this child also creates).
  - criteria: [G1G2]
  - location: plan "What" §1 ("ALL 7 specs"); AC line 1 ("the 7 specs")
  - evidence: `list_directory src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` → 4 `.eval.yaml` files (finder, verifier, isf-finder, code-quality) + README; README.plan-review.md:8-12 tabulates only 3 plan-review specs. ABSENCE of any 5th–7th `.eval.yaml` makes "7 specs" an unenumerated/over-counted edit-target set.
  - suggested_fix: (empty — clarify whether "7" means 7 prompts/criteria-overlays vs. the 4 shipped eval specs; the right number depends on author intent.)

- [ ] Enumerate the OUT-OF-DIR consumers the validate_scorer change breaks, and pre-load the registry with the shipped specs' names so existing gates stay green. Tightening `validate_scorer`/`validate_eval_spec` to FAIL an unregistered deterministic name is high blast-radius because `load_eval_spec` calls `validate_eval_spec` and RAISES on any error (eval.py:87-90) — so the moment a spec carries a name not yet in the registry, every consumer of `load_eval_spec` breaks: the offline CI gate (.github/workflows/eval.yml:47-50, which validates ALL packaged specs), tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:28/107, and tests/unit/test_eval.py:21/258. Concretely, tests/unit/test_eval.py:83 ASSERTS `validate_scorer({"type":"deterministic","name":"x"}) == []` — an arbitrary unregistered name currently PASSES; the proposed change inverts that contract and will FAIL this existing test unless it is updated. The plan does not enumerate these consumers or the required test edit.
  - criteria: [G1G2]
  - location: plan "What" §3 ("Extend validate_eval_spec/validate_scorer so an unregistered/typo'd deterministic scorer name FAILS the offline gate"); AC line 2
  - evidence: load_eval_spec raises on validate errors (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:87-90); validate_scorer's deterministic branch currently returns [] for any name (eval.py:120-123); existing test asserts name "x" passes (tests/unit/test_eval.py:83-84); offline gate validates all packaged specs (.github/workflows/eval.yml:47-50); plan-review eval tests call load_eval_spec/validate_eval_spec (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:28,107). High blast-radius is acceptable IF acknowledged — the gap is that it is unacknowledged and unenumerated.
  - suggested_fix: In the plan, (a) list these consumers (test_eval.py:83, .github/workflows/eval.yml, test_plan_review_evals.py) as edits the change requires, and (b) make pre-registering the 11 shipped deterministic scorer names an explicit prerequisite of the validation tightening so existing specs/CI stay green.
